mapping.a_element: spatially-gridded map of telecom activity counts (100m x 155m cells)
mapping.b_element: multi-band pixel map of unresolved background light

mapping.a_element: city-wide/leisure-vs-service activity quantities inferred per area
mapping.b_element: flux-weighted redshift distribution inferred per map/tomographic bin

brief_justification: The only real correspondence is that both represent data as spatial maps/grids; the governing machinery differs. Paper A is a descriptive spatiotemporal analysis that aggregates telecom counts and compares them to an expected baseline conditioned on weather and point-of-interest covariates, whereas Paper B's core is a trained conditional normalising flow performing probabilistic density estimation of redshift distributions with data imputation — a generative ML inference method with no counterpart in A, so no role-for-role sharing of the estimation mechanism holds.
